result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would dismantle the illusion of separateness.

I would make every person feel—viscerally and unquestionably—the deep, living connection between their own well-being and the well-being of everything else: the stranger on the other side of the planet, the animal in the forest, the health of the soil, the stability of the climate.

This isn’t about adding more information or ethics; it’s about altering the fundamental perception of reality. If that shift happened, most of our destructive systems would crumble from irrelevance. Conflict, exploitation, and indifference would become as absurd as starving yourself while desperately trying to feed your own hand.

The change wouldn’t be in laws or technology, but in the felt experience of existence: from “me against the world” to “me as part of the world.” Everything else—justice, sustainability, peace—would follow from that one transformed root.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
